---
description: "Run the pre-release checklist for the Electron desktop app. Use before tagging a release to verify build matrix, code signing, changelog, auto-update config, and distribution artifacts are all correct."
argument-hint: "Version number to release (e.g. 1.2.0)"
agent: "agent"
tools: [read, search, execute]
---

Run the pre-release checklist for version **{{userInput}}** of this Electron desktop application.

Work through each section below. For each item: check the actual state of the codebase/config, report ✅ PASS, ❌ FAIL, or ⚠️ WARN with a short explanation, and suggest a fix for anything that is not passing.

---

## 1. Version Consistency

- [ ] `package.json` `version` field matches `{{userInput}}`
- [ ] `electron-builder` config version (if separate) matches
- [ ] No hardcoded version strings in source code that differ

## 2. Changelog

- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` has an entry for `{{userInput}}`
- [ ] Entry includes: release date, new features, bug fixes, breaking changes
- [ ] Previous version entry is still intact (no accidental overwrites)

## 3. TypeScript & Lint

- [ ] `tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors
- [ ] ESLint passes with zero errors (warnings acceptable, document them)
- [ ] No `// @ts-ignore` or `// eslint-disable` added since last release without justification

## 4. Security Checks

- [ ] No `nodeIntegration: true` anywhere in BrowserWindow config
- [ ] No `contextIsolation: false` anywhere
- [ ] No `webSecurity: false` anywhere
- [ ] All IPC channel inputs validated with zod schemas
- [ ] No secrets or API tokens committed to source
- [ ] `shell.openExternal` calls only accept validated `https://` URLs

## 5. Tests

- [ ] All unit tests pass (`vitest run`)
- [ ] All integration tests pass
- [ ] E2E tests pass on at least one platform (document which)
- [ ] Test coverage has not regressed from the previous release

## 6. Build Matrix

Verify the build produces valid artifacts for each target:

- [ ] Windows: `.exe` (NSIS installer)
- [ ] macOS: `.dmg` (universal or arm64 + x64)
- [ ] Linux: `.AppImage`
- [ ] Linux: `.deb`

## 7. Code Signing

- [ ] Windows: Authenticode signing configured (or documented as skipped for dev builds)
- [ ] macOS: App is signed and notarized (or documented as skipped)
- [ ] Linux: GPG signing for `.deb`/`.AppImage` (if applicable)

## 8. Auto-Update Configuration

- [ ] `electron-updater` `publish` config points to the correct GitHub repo / update server
- [ ] Update channel (`latest`, `beta`, `alpha`) is correct for this release
- [ ] `latest.yml` / `latest-mac.yml` / `latest-linux.yml` will be generated by the build
- [ ] Differential update (blockmap) enabled if using `electron-updater`

## 9. GitHub Release

- [ ] Git tag `v{{userInput}}` does not already exist
- [ ] Release branch / commit is clean (no uncommitted changes)
- [ ] GitHub Actions CI pipeline is passing on the release branch
- [ ] Release notes drafted (can be generated from CHANGELOG entry)

## 10. Dependency Audit

- [ ] `npm audit` shows no critical or high vulnerabilities
- [ ] All dependencies are pinned or have acceptable version ranges
- [ ] No deprecated packages with known security issues

---

## Summary Report

After checking all items, produce a summary table:

| Section             | Status | Blockers |
| ------------------- | ------ | -------- |
| Version Consistency |        |          |
| Changelog           |        |          |
| TypeScript & Lint   |        |          |
| Security            |        |          |
| Tests               |        |          |
| Build Matrix        |        |          |
| Code Signing        |        |          |
| Auto-Update         |        |          |
| GitHub Release      |        |          |
| Dependencies        |        |          |

**Overall status**: READY TO RELEASE / BLOCKED (list blockers)

If there are any ❌ FAIL items, list concrete commands or file edits needed to resolve them before the release can proceed.
